description: Update the zeroupload-brand-system SKILL.md to document ATELIER as the new design system. Replace 'Quiet Luxury' references with 'Atelier'. Document both Porcelain (light) and Vault (dark) palettes, the new typography stack (Fraunces + Geist + Geist Mono), the brass accent rules, signature elements (glow, shimmer, offline-proof pill, mono timing), and component patterns.
steps: In .kiro/skills/zeroupload-brand-system/SKILL.md: Update the aesthetic name from 'Quiet Luxury' to 'Atelier'. Keep the motto, mandatory requirements, and brand essence (calm, confident, premium, trustworthy) unchanged. Update 'Design tokens' section to document both Porcelain and Vault palettes with their oklch values. Document Brass rules: rare highlights/hovers/numbers ONLY, never for small body text (fails contrast), always paired with Ink or Pine for readable text. Document the typography: Fraunces (display), Geist (body), Geist Mono (technical/numbers/timings/format labels). Document signature elements: on-device glow, success shimmer, mono timing readouts, offline-proof pill, hover-lift + brass underline on cards. Document dark mode toggle behavior (localStorage persistence, [data-theme] attribute). Update Do/Don't to reflect Atelier.
Run `npm run build` to confirm no impact (skill files are not compiled, just documentation).
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md documents ATELIER as the current design system
Both Porcelain (light) and Vault (dark) palettes are documented with hex + oklch values
Brass usage rules are clearly stated (rare, never small text)
Typography stack (Fraunces + Geist + Geist Mono) is documented with use cases
Signature elements are listed and described
Mandatory requirements and motto remain unchanged
verification: Run `npm run build` in /projects/sandbox/ZeroUpload and confirm exit code 0
findings: Documentation-only change. npm run build cannot fully execute due to proxy/environment constraints (same as FEAT-001/002), but SKILL.md is not compiled - it is a steering file read by agents. All acceptance criteria met: Atelier documented as the design system, both palettes with oklch+hex, brass rules, typography, signature elements, dark mode, and Do/Don't updated.
